articles.title: House passes bill extending protections for Haitian migrants in the U.S.
articles.summary: Ten Republicans voted alongside Democrats, in a rebuke to the Trump administration's immigration policies. Should it pass the Senate, the White House said President Trump would veto the measure.

articles.title: Top five takeaways from Homeland Security budget hearings
articles.summary: Lawmakers have been in a stalemate for over 60 days about funding the entire department, which includes agencies that oversee immigration enforcement, disaster relief, cybersecurity and the U.S. Coast

articles.title: D4vd Arrested in Connection With Death of Celeste Rivas Hernandez
articles.summary: The singer was detained in connection with the death of Celeste Rivas Hernandez, who died before her 15th birthday. D4vd’s lawyers said they would vigorously defend his innocence.

articles.title: Who Are D4vd and Celeste Rivas Hernandez?
articles.summary: The musician is known for creating the anthem for Fortnite. He was touring for his major-label debut when Ms. Rivas Hernandez’s remains were found in his car, officials said.

articles.title: Analilia Mejia, a Progressive Democrat, Wins Mikie Sherrill’s House Seat
articles.summary: Ms. Mejia, who helped run Sen. Bernie Sanders’s presidential campaign, beat her Republican opponent, Joe Hathaway, to win a seat Ms. Sherrill vacated after she was elected governor of New Jersey.

articles.title: Singer D4vd is arrested months after a teen's remains were found in his car
articles.summary: The 21-year-old Houston-born singer, whose real name is David Burke, had been under a secret investigation by an LA County grand jury after a 14-year-old girl's decomposed body was found in his car.

articles.title: These musicians are providing the soundtrack for anti-ICE protests in LA
articles.summary: Los Jornaleros Del Norte play protest songs whose lyrics reflect the hopes and struggles of undocumented workers as they evade immigration agents patrolling the streets.

articles.title: Minnesota has charged an ICE officer with assault for alleged actions during immigration surge
articles.summary: Hennepin County officials say these are the first charges filed against a federal immigration agent related to the crackdown that brought thousands of federal officers to the state. The widespread ope

articles.title: Acting ICE Director Says He Plans to Resign in May
articles.summary: Todd Lyons said he would leave to spend more time with his family. He has spoken about a surge in threats against ICE officers, saying that he knew the reality firsthand.

articles.title: RFK Jr. Shifts Tone on Vaccines in Congressional Hearing
articles.summary: Robert F. Kennedy Jr., who has sought to roll back longstanding vaccine policy, testified that the measles vaccine is safe and effective “for most people” and agreed it was safer than getting measles.

articles.title: Trump to Nominate Dr. Erica Schwartz, a Vaccine Supporter, as CDC Director
articles.summary: Dr. Erica Schwartz is seen as a highly qualified traditional choice and tapping her is the strongest signal yet that the administration is veering away from vaccine skepticism this election year.

articles.title: Trump to Pick Ousted FEMA Head to Lead Agency Again
articles.summary: Cameron Hamilton is a former Navy SEAL who ran unsuccessfully for Congress.

articles.title: Israel and Lebanon Agree to a Cease-Fire Deal
articles.summary: Also, Utah is now the epicenter of U.S. measles cases. Here’s the latest at the end of Thursday.

articles.title: Six months after ceasefire with Israel, people in Gaza say recovery hasn't even begun
articles.summary: Six months into a ceasefire that promised an end to the war and a surge of aid for Gaza, people say recovery hasn't even yet begun.

articles.title: UN looks for a short-term solution for moving fertilizer through the Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: The U.N. looks for a quick solution to get cargo ships with fertilizer through the Strait of Hormuz, to prevent a global food crisis.

articles.title: Trump nominates former Coast Guard doctor as CDC chief
articles.summary: The nomination comes after months of interim leadership at the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention.

articles.title: How the conflict in Iran is testing the U.S.-European alliance
articles.summary: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly speaks with correspondents Greg Myre and Rob Schmitz about how the Iran war is affecting the U.S. alliance with Europe.

articles.title: A jury declared Live Nation a monopoly. But ticket prices won't drop just yet
articles.summary: D.C. and 33 states now have to argue in favor of specific remedies and fines, which could be paused if Live Nation appeals. Experts say the long-term impact on ticket prices isn't clear either.

articles.title: Israeli and Palestinian activists share a vision for peace in Gaza
articles.summary: Maoz Inon's parents were killed by Hamas in the Oct. 7 attacks. Aziz Abu Sarah's brother died after being tortured in an Israeli military prison. Their new book is The Future Is Peace.

articles.title: A complex set of negotiations to end Israel's overlapping wars
articles.summary: Israel has agreed to a ceasefire in Lebanon, part of a complex web of Mideast negotiations, from Iran to Gaza.

articles.title: Pope Leo takes aim at  'handful of tyrants' spending billions on war amid tensions with Trump
articles.summary: Pope Leo XIV condemns "tyrants" fueling war with billions. His calls for global peace during his Africa trip come amid rising tensions with President Trump.

articles.title: Iran War timeline continues to shift. And, jury rules Live Nation acted as monopoly
articles.summary: Trump continues to try to declare victory in Iran as the timeline for the war's end shifts. And, a jury determined that Live Nation acted as a monopoly and overcharged ticket buyers.

articles.title: Israel starts a tense ceasefire in Lebanon, as Trump sounds optimistic on Iran talks
articles.summary: The leaders of Israel and Lebanon agreed to a 10-day ceasefire in Israel's fight against Iran-backed Hezbollah in Lebanon, but Israel vowed to keep troops in southern Lebanon.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
